approvalTitle: The Open Book — Tasks (2 waves: spread shell/atoms → marginalia/spread/dispatch)
content:
# Tasks — The Open Book (desktop two-page spread)

Execution: TWO SEQUENCED WAVES. Wave 1 stands up the spread shell +
selection + the atom treatments (the structural foundation); Wave 2 fills
the pages (marginalia, footnotes, page-turn) and wires ReaderScreen. Every
agent MUST read `design.md` §0 (OB1–OB9) before its first edit. Client-only;
no convex/engine/server files anywhere in this spec. Merge gate per wave:
monorepo typecheck + `pnpm --filter @cyoa/app test` + touched vitest, then
`docker compose restart app` and a live check at 1440px AND 375px (the phone
must stay byte-identical — OB9). Conventions: conditional-spread optionals;
no control emoji (icon font / text); pure helpers carry `.test.mjs`;
drift-guards updated with the surface they guard.

---

# WAVE 1 — Spread shell, selection, and atom treatments

## Agent OB-SELECT — owns `apps/app/lib/responsive.ts` + `apps/app/components/reading/ReaderScreen.tsx` (resolveActiveLayout + dispatch only) + `apps/app/hooks/useReaderSettings.ts` (variant union) + tests

- [ ] 1.1 Wide-select + register the variant (R1, OB2/OB3)
  Add `SPREAD_MIN = 1024` + `SPREAD_MAX = 1400` + an `isWide` flag (width ≥
  SPREAD_MIN) to `lib/responsive.ts`. Add `spread` to the
  `ReaderLayoutVariant` union + `READER_LAYOUT_VARIANTS` + `isLayoutVariant`
  in `useReaderSettings.ts`. Extend `resolveActiveLayout`
  (`ReaderScreen.tsx:79-88`) to take the viewport `width` (from
  `useBreakpoint`, which already returns it) and return `spread` when
  `width >= SPREAD_MIN && !hasExplicitLayoutOverride()`; phone→mobile still
  applies first; explicit pick always wins (R1.2–R1.4). Do NOT yet render
  Spread (task 1.2 builds it; here just make the resolver + union ready and
  guarded so a missing Spread falls back to book). Tests: the wide-select
  matrix (phone / 768–1023 / ≥1024 × override on/off × stored layout).
  - _Leverage: `ReaderScreen.tsx:79-88` (resolveActiveLayout + hasExplicitLayoutOverride), `:495-505` (dispatch), `lib/responsive.ts:31-58` (breakpoint flags + width), `hooks/useReaderSettings.ts` (ReaderLayoutVariant union)_
  - _Requirements: R1.1, R1.2, R1.3, R1.4_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec open-book, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: React Native client developer. Task: add SPREAD_MIN/SPREAD_MAX/isWide, register the spread variant, and extend resolveActiveLayout to wide-select per design §2 and OB2/OB3. Restrictions: reuse the existing explicit-override machinery (do not invent a second flag); phone override precedence unchanged; explicit pick always wins; spread never auto-selects <1024. Success: .test.mjs green over the full selection matrix. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

## Agent OB-ATOMS — owns `apps/app/components/reading/ProseRenderer.tsx` + `apps/app/components/reading/layouts/spread/FootnoteChoices.tsx` (new) + `apps/app/components/reading/layouts/spread/pageTurnAnim.ts` (new) + tests

- [ ] 1.2 Drop-cap prose + footnote choices + page-turn driver (R4, R5, R6)
  Add an OPTIONAL `dropCap?: boolean` to `ProseRenderer` (absent ⇒ today's
  render, byte-identical — OB6); when true, the first settled paragraph's
  first letter renders enlarged in the accent, spanning ~2–3 serif lines,
  degrading cleanly for a short scene (R4.2). Build `FootnoteChoices`
  (new) — a numbered footnote list over `ChoiceProjection[]` + the unchanged
  `onChoose` (locked rows / free-form / check chips reused as thin pieces);
  Novel mode (`readingMode === "novel"`) collapses to the single page-turn
  affordance (reuse `layouts/pageTurn.ts`). Build `pageTurnAnim.ts` — the
  pure `shouldAnimatePageTurn(reducedMotion)` + a small Animated driver.
  `ChoiceList` stays byte-identical (do NOT edit it). Tests: dropCap split +
  short-scene degradation; FootnoteChoices submits via onChoose + Novel
  page-turn; shouldAnimatePageTurn matrix.
  - _Leverage: `ProseRenderer.tsx` (prose render + the parseProseToBlocks memo), `choices/ChoiceList.tsx` (locked/free-form/check pieces to mirror thin), `layouts/pageTurn.ts` (PAGE_TURN_CHOICE_ID/resolvePageTurnChoice), `layouts/Novel.tsx` (page-turn affordance reference)_
  - _Requirements: R4.1, R4.2, R4.3, R5.1, R5.2, R5.3, R6.1, R6.2_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec open-book, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: React Native client developer. Task: add the optional dropCap treatment, build FootnoteChoices, and the pure page-turn driver per design §1 and R4/R5/R6. Restrictions: ProseRenderer absent-dropCap is byte-identical; ChoiceList untouched (footnotes are a NEW thin component over the same model); onChoose path unchanged (no pipeline fork); Novel collapses to the page-turn; reduced-motion snaps. Success: .test.mjs green over dropCap, footnote submit, Novel collapse, and the animate matrix. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

## MERGE-GATE (Wave 1)

- [ ] 1.3 Gate + live check
  Typecheck + app suite + touched vitest; `docker compose restart app`;
  confirm at 375px the reader is BYTE-IDENTICAL (no spread), and that the
  new variant/atoms compile and are reachable (a temporary manual spread
  pick renders book+dropCap without crashing). No visual spread yet — that
  is Wave 2.
  - _Requirements: R7.2, OB9_

---

# WAVE 2 — Fill the pages + wire the spread

## Agent OB-MARGIN — owns `apps/app/components/reading/layouts/spread/Marginalia.tsx` (new) + tests

- [ ] 2.1 The verso marginalia rail (R3, OB5)
  Build `Marginalia` — the verso margin rendering the SAME signals the
  declutter StoryRibbon composes (pursuit / threads / doors / daily pulse /
  stats + the two-stage candle), as quiet margin notes, reusing the EXISTING
  components/data hooks (no new queries; each self-hides on its predicate;
  all absent ⇒ empty rail so the illustration takes the page — RC2). Interactive
  destinations (threads/doors detail, patronage door) unchanged. Tests: each
  note self-hides on its predicate; all-absent ⇒ empty; the candle two-stage
  note.
  - _Leverage: `chrome/StoryRibbon.tsx` (the signal composition to mirror as margin notes), `chrome/ribbonSegments.ts` (pursuit/candle rules), `reading/QuestLine.tsx` / `ThreadsPill.tsx` / `DoorsJournal.tsx` / `daily/DailyPulseChip.tsx` / `stats/StatsHud.tsx`_
  - _Requirements: R3.1, R3.2, R3.3_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec open-book, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: React Native client developer. Task: build the verso Marginalia rail per design §1/§3 and OB5. Restrictions: reuse the existing signal components/data — no new queries, no re-derived predicates (RC2); all-absent renders empty; the candle two-stage rule matches the declutter; margin-note styling stays quiet reading furniture. Success: .test.mjs green over the per-note self-hide matrix. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

## Agent OB-SPREAD — owns `apps/app/components/reading/layouts/Spread.tsx` (new) + `apps/app/components/reading/layouts/index.ts` (register) + `apps/app/components/reading/ReaderScreen.tsx` (spread dispatch + width thread) + tests

- [ ] 2.2 The Spread layout + registration + dispatch (R2, R7, OB1/OB4/OB7/OB8)
  Build `Spread.tsx` consuming the UNCHANGED `ReaderLayoutProps`: desk
  ground; verso (SceneMedia plate + `<Marginalia>`), spine, recto
  (`<ProseRenderer dropCap>` + `<FootnoteChoices>`); capped ≤ SPREAD_MAX,
  centered; single-page fallback < SPREAD_MIN (R1.4/R2.2). Terminal → the
  EXISTING `EndingPanel` centered (OB7). Novel → FootnoteChoices' page-turn
  drives the transition (OB8). Register `READER_LAYOUTS.spread`. In
  ReaderScreen, thread `width` into `resolveActiveLayout` and let `spread`
  win dispatch (it reads `readingMode` itself for Novel — design §2). Keep
  the ReaderScreen chrome (top bar + ribbon) in the 760 column ABOVE the
  spread; only the layout region widens (OB4). Do NOT disturb the declutter
  chrome, Novel/daily/act/narration/Focus wiring already in ReaderScreen.
  Tests: Spread consumes ReaderLayoutProps with no added fields (no-fork
  pin); fallback < SPREAD_MIN; terminal renders EndingPanel; dispatch selects
  spread ≥1024 unless overridden.
  - _Leverage: `layouts/Mobile.tsx` (the inner-chrome reference: SceneMedia/ProseRenderer/EndingPanel/ChoiceList mount order), `layouts/types.ts:13` (ReaderLayoutProps — DO NOT change), `ReaderScreen.tsx:495-505` (dispatch), `Marginalia`/`FootnoteChoices`/`pageTurnAnim` from Wave 1 + 2.1_
  - _Requirements: R2.1, R2.2, R2.3, R2.4, R6.3, R7.1, R7.2, R7.3_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec open-book, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: React Native client developer on the reading surface. Task: build Spread.tsx, register it, and wire the ReaderScreen dispatch per design §1/§2/§3 and OB1/OB4/OB7/OB8. Restrictions: identical ReaderLayoutProps — no pipeline fork (OB1); spread overrides only the layout column cap, chrome stays in 760 (OB4); terminal uses the existing EndingPanel (OB7); Novel collapses to the page-turn (OB8); do not disturb the declutter/Novel/daily/act/narration/Focus wiring in ReaderScreen; phone/<1024 byte-identical. Success: tests green (no-fork pin, fallback, terminal, dispatch); 1440px shows the spread, 375px unchanged. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

## MERGE-GATE (Wave 2)

- [ ] 2.3 Gate + live verification
  Full typecheck + app suite + touched vitest; `docker compose restart app`;
  live check on cyoa-local: at 1440px a wide reader shows the two-page
  spread (illustration+margin | drop-cap prose + footnotes), the page-turn
  animates on advance (and snaps under reduced-motion), a Novel save shows
  the single page-turn, a terminal shows the ending panel; at 375px the
  reader is byte-identical to today; a reader who explicitly picks another
  layout is not auto-spread. Record the wide-reader observation.
  - _Requirements: R2, R6, R7, OB9_
